message:
fix(algorithms,readwrite): correct transitive_closure self-loop behavior and GML node ID assignment

transitive_closure: Add `reflexive` parameter (default false) to control
self-loop insertion. Previously, self-loops were unconditionally added for
every node. Now self-loops are only preserved when they exist in the input
graph, or added for all nodes when reflexive=true. Updates Python binding
to expose the new parameter via pyo3 signature. Updates Rust unit tests
to match the corrected semantics.

EdgeListEngine (GML write): Fix node ID collision when graphs contain a
mix of integer-parseable and non-integer node names. Uses a two-pass
approach: first pass reserves parsed integer IDs, second pass assigns
remaining nodes to unused sequential IDs, preventing overwrites.

EdgeListEngine (GML read): Replace silent fallback to 0 on unparseable
node IDs with an explicit error via FailClosed, surfacing malformed GML
data instead of silently corrupting the graph.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
